zh.Strensham, Worcestershire, England: 英國武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆
zh.Kingston Russell, Dorset, England: 英國多瑟特金斯頓 羅素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Odo de Russell於大約1150年出生在英國多瑟特貝維其可時，他的父親Sir Robert de Russell II27歲，母親Anne Sullivan22歲。他於1180年之前與Alica Bardolf在英國武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1230年在英國武斯特斯爾享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Odo de Russell于大约1150年出生在英国多瑟特貝維其可时，他的父亲Sir Robert de Russell II27岁，母亲Anne Sullivan22岁。他于1180年之前与Alica Bardolf在英国武斯特斯爾斯特倫善姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1230年在英国武斯特斯爾享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Russell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: of Norman origin, from Old French and Anglo-Norman French r(o)ussel, a diminutive of Old French rous(e) ‘red, reddish’, used either as a nickname for someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion, or as a personal name. Compare Rouse . This Norman name has been established in Ireland since the 12th century. It has been reinforced in Britain and Ireland by Huguenot bearers of the name Roussel, of the same Old French origin.

English: habitational name from any of several places called Rushall (Norfolk, Staffordshire, Wiltshire) or possibly sometimes from Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent). Rushall in Staffordshire derives from Old English rysc ‘rush, rushes’ + halh ‘nook, corner of land’. Rushall in Norfolk derives from an uncertain first element + Old English halh. Rushall in Wiltshire derives from an Old English personal name Rust (genitive Rustes) + halh. Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent) probably derives from Old English rust ‘rust, rust color’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’, but with a change in the final element due to influence from Middle English, Old English hall ‘hall, residence’, perhaps referring to a nearby building.
Americanized form of German Rüssel, from a pet form of any of various personal names formed with the element hrōd ‘fame, renown’.

variants.name: Russler
meanings: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of rüsseln ‘to shallow plow’.

variants.name: Rouse
meanings: English: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French rous(e) ‘red(-haired)’ (from Latin russ(e)us).
Walloon and northern French (Rousé): variant of Rosé (see Rose ).

variants.name: Russel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Russell  .
German: from a pet form of Russ, see Russ 2.
northwest German (Rüssel): probably a variant of Rossel .

nameMeanings.name: Odo
meanings: the Celtic name Aodh has been Anglicised into Hugh, and Latinised variously Hugo, Ædus, Ædanus, Aidus, and Odo.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Odo de Russell was born about 1150, in Berwick, Dorset, England, his father, Sir Robert de Russell II, was 27 and his mother, Anne Sullivan, was 22. He married Alica Bardolf before 1180, in Strensham, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1230, in Worcestershire, England, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Odo de Russell est né environ 1150, à Berwick, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert de Russell II, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Anne Sullivan, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Alica Bardolf avant 1180, à Strensham, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1230, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Odo de Russell um 1150 in Berwick, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert de Russell II 27 und seine Mutter Anne Sullivan 22. Er hat vor 1180 in Strensham, Worcestershire, England Alica Bardolf geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1230 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Worcestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Odo de Russell è nato il circa 1150 nel Berwick, Dorset, England, il suo padre, Sir Robert de Russell II, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Anne Sullivan, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Alica Bardolf prima del 1180, nel Strensham, Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1230, nel Worcestershire, England, all'età di 82 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Odo de Russellが約1150年にイギリスドールセトバーウィックで生まれた時，父Sir Robert de Russell IIは27歳，母Anne Sullivanは22歳であった。1180年以前にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアストレンシャムでAlica Bardolfと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は約1230年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Odo de Russell(이)가 대략 1150년 영국 도르세트 버윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert de Russell II(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Sullivan(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1180년 이전 영국 워스스터실르 스트렌샤에서 Alica Bardolf(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1230년 영국 워스스터실르에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Odo de Russell nasceu aproximadamente 1150, em Berwick, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert de Russell II, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Anne Sullivan, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Alica Bardolf antes 1180, em Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1230, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Odo de Russell родился около 1150 г. в местности бирвикк, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert de Russell II было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Sullivan, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Alica Bardolf перед 1180 г. в местности стреншам, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1230 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет.

sm.about 1150–about 1230: e tusa o le 1150–e tusa o le 1230

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Odo de Russell nació aproximadamente 1150, en Berwick, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert de Russell II, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Anne Sullivan, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Alica Bardolf antes de 1180, en Strensham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1230, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 82 años.
